title: Erasmus Mundus Joint Masters Scholarships
host_countries: Multiple countries in Europe and beyond
manual_review_requirements: Confirm that the selected consortium programme is currently offered and accepts scholarship applications.
Verify the programme-specific academic field, admission criteria, language requirements, documents, and exact deadline.
Confirm that the applicant holds a bachelor's degree or officially recognised equivalent and will graduate before the master's programme starts.
eligibility_text: Students from all over the world may apply directly to the institution running a chosen Erasmus Mundus Joint Master. Applicants must hold a bachelor's degree, be in their final bachelor year and graduate before the programme starts, or demonstrate an officially recognised equivalent. Full scholarships may be offered to best-ranked students and cover participation costs while contributing to travel, visa, and a living allowance. Most applications are submitted between October and January, but each programme publishes its own requirements and deadline.

title: SI Scholarship for Global Professionals
manual_review_requirements: Verify that the applicant's citizenship appears on the current official list of 34 eligible countries.
Verify that the selected Swedish master's programme is eligible for the SI scholarship.
Confirm tuition-fee liability through University Admissions.
Verify the official work-experience and leadership-evidence requirements.
Check the next application cycle because the inspected 2026 scholarship round is closed.
eligibility_text: The scholarship supports full-time one- or two-year master's studies at Swedish universities. Applicants must be citizens of one of 34 eligible countries, apply to an eligible master's programme, be liable for tuition fees, and demonstrate work and leadership experience. The inspected 2026 application round closed on 25 February 2026. Funding includes full tuition coverage, a monthly allowance of SEK 12,000, and a one-time travel grant subject to the official conditions.

title: University of Twente Scholarship
manual_review_requirements: Confirm provisional admission to a qualifying University of Twente master's programme and possession of a student number.
Verify non-EU/EEA status and confirm that the applicant is not Dutch, an EU/EEA student, a current UT student, or a UT graduate.
Verify visa eligibility and the IELTS 6.0 speaking subscore or TOEFL iBT 20 speaking requirement.
Confirm that the applicant is among the best 5 to 10 percent of the graduating class.
Check programme and faculty exclusions on the official scholarship page.
eligibility_text: The University of Twente Scholarship supports excellent non-EU/EEA students applying to qualifying University of Twente MSc programmes. Its listed value is EUR 3,000 to EUR 22,000 for one year and is compensation for study-related costs rather than direct payment of tuition or living costs. Applicants need prior provisional admission, must satisfy visa and English-language requirements, and should belong to the best 5 to 10 percent of their graduating class. The inspected application deadline is 1 April 2027.

title: Adelaide Academic Excellence Scholarship
manual_review_requirements: Confirm that the applicant is an international student under Adelaide University's current definition.
Confirm that the intended CRICOS-registered bachelor's or master's degree satisfies the stated minimum duration after any credit or recognised prior learning.
Confirm that the intended degree is not one of the excluded programmes listed on the official scholarship page.
Confirm that the applicant is not an Australia Awards recipient.
Confirm that the applicant does not hold an international sponsorship tuition-fee waiver equal to or greater than the scholarship value.
Confirm selection by the scholarship panel because the award is competitive and places are limited.
eligibility_text: Adelaide University's Adelaide Academic Excellence Scholarship provides a 50% tuition fee reduction for commencing international students applying to qualifying CRICOS-registered bachelor or master coursework degrees. Applicants using GPA for assessment require a minimum overall GPA of 6.7 on a 7.0 scale or a comparable result. The official page excludes specified programmes, Australia Awards recipients, and certain sponsored students. Semester 2 2026 applications closed on 22 May 2026.

title: Allan and Nesta Ferguson Charitable Trust Masters Scholarships
manual_review_requirements: Verify that the applicant's country appears on the official eligible-countries list.
Confirm that the applicant held an offer for an eligible University of Sheffield course by 8 May 2026.
Confirm overseas tuition-fee status.
Confirm that the applicant does not already hold an equivalent or higher-level qualification.
Confirm compliance with the official previous-study restriction.
Confirm that the applicant was shortlisted and invited to submit the scholarship application.
Confirm competitive selection based on academic strength, rationale, work experience and future ambitions.
eligibility_text: The University of Sheffield and the Allan and Nesta Ferguson Charitable Trust offered three scholarships for eligible developing-country students on specified master's courses in 2026. Each award provided a full tuition fee waiver and a GBP 12,500 maintenance stipend. Applicants needed an eligible course offer, overseas fee status and successful competitive selection. The invited application deadline was 1 June 2026.

title: ANU PhD Scholarship
manual_review_requirements: Confirm admission or a complete application for admission to an ANU PhD or Professional Doctorate by Research programme.
Verify that the applicant's previous qualification satisfies the stated first-class honours, research master's or recognised-equivalent requirement.
Confirm the applicable international, domestic or later scholarship-round deadline for the intended commencement.
Confirm that all supporting documents and referee reports were complete when the admission application was submitted.
Confirm competitive selection based on academic merit and the relevant ANU college ranking process.
Verify tuition-fee liability and any separate tuition scholarship because the inspected page explicitly describes a stipend and allowances but not a universal tuition-fee waiver.
eligibility_text: The ANU PhD Scholarship supports domestic and international postgraduate research students across all fields of study. The official page lists a 2026 base stipend of AUD 39,069 per year, paid fortnightly for 3.5 years, with additional allowances. Applicants must apply for admission to an ANU PhD or Professional Doctorate by Research and normally hold first-class honours, a research master's qualification or a recognised equivalent. Scholarship round deadlines vary by applicant category and intended commencement.

title: ETH Excellence Scholarship and Opportunity Programme
manual_review_requirements: Confirm application to an eligible ETH Zurich master's programme.
Verify that the applicant's bachelor results place them approximately in the top 10 percent of their programme.
Verify all required academic and programme-specific application documents.
Confirm that the intended master's programme lasts three or four semesters.
Confirm final competitive selection by ETH Zurich.
eligibility_text: ETH Zurich's Excellence Scholarship and Opportunity Programme supports outstanding master's students with full study and living costs. The official page states CHF 12,000 per semester plus a tuition-fee waiver for the regular three- or four-semester programme duration. Applicants are expected to rank in approximately the top 10 percent of their bachelor programme. The 2027 application window closes on 30 November 2026.

title: KTH Scholarship
manual_review_requirements: Confirm that the applicant is liable to pay tuition fees in Sweden.
Confirm that an eligible KTH master's programme was ranked as the applicant's first priority.
Confirm admission eligibility for the selected KTH master's programme.
Verify the academic-excellence and programme-specific selection factors.
Confirm that the scholarship application was complete before the official deadline.
eligibility_text: The KTH Scholarship covers the full tuition fee for an eligible KTH master's programme but does not include living costs. Applicants must be fee-paying students and must select an eligible KTH programme as their first priority. Selection considers academic excellence and other programme factors. The 2026 application deadline was 15 January 2026.

title: University of Auckland Doctoral Scholarship
manual_review_requirements: Confirm an eligible offer or place in a University of Auckland doctoral programme.
Confirm the correct domestic or international application round and deadline.
Verify compliance with the doctoral admission and scholarship-ranking requirements.
Confirm full-time doctoral enrolment and the applicable award duration.
Confirm final selection under the competitive scholarship scheme.
eligibility_text: The University of Auckland Doctoral Scholarship is awarded through a competitive scheme with four rounds each year. The 2026 award provides a NZD 35,468 annual stipend, tuition fees for up to 42 months and the compulsory single-student health-insurance charge for eligible international recipients. Applicants must be offered a place in an eligible doctoral programme and meet the relevant round requirements.

title: Think Big Scholarships
manual_review_requirements: Confirm the applicant has applied for an eligible University of Bristol course starting in September 2026.
Confirm the applicant is classed as an overseas student for fee purposes.
Confirm the selected course appears in the official Think Big eligible-course list.
eligibility_text: The University of Bristol Think Big scheme provides funding towards tuition fees for international students studying eligible undergraduate or postgraduate courses. Applicants must have applied for an eligible course beginning in September 2026 and must be classed as overseas students for fee purposes. Applications close at 10:00 UK time on 10 April 2026.

title: Global Leadership Scholarship
manual_review_requirements: Confirm the applicant has International or EU fee status.
Confirm the applicant holds an offer for an eligible full-time, one-year, in-person postgraduate taught master's programme starting in September 2026.
Confirm the applicant demonstrates academic excellence equivalent to UK first-class honours.
Confirm the proposed qualification and programme are not excluded by the official scholarship rules.
eligibility_text: The University of Glasgow Global Leadership Scholarship is a one-year tuition-fee discount for International fee students beginning an eligible postgraduate taught master's programme in any discipline in academic session 2026/2027. Applicants must demonstrate academic excellence equivalent to UK first-class honours and hold an eligible full-time programme offer. The stated deadline is 17 July 2026.

title: Aalto University Excellence Scholarship
manual_review_requirements: Confirm the applicant is liable to pay tuition fees and applied for the scholarship during the admission process.
Confirm the applicant satisfies the selected programme's admission and scholarship evaluation criteria.
Confirm the applicant is selected among the highest-achieving candidates through the competitive programme assessment.
Confirm the applicant accepts all scholarship terms by the programme-specific deadline.
Confirm the applicant can maintain the required full-time study progress for continuation in later academic years.
eligibility_text: Aalto University Excellence Scholarships are competitive merit-based tuition-fee waivers covering the full tuition fee. Tuition-fee students apply during admission to an eligible bachelor's or master's programme. The award does not cover living or other study-related costs. Programme-specific selection criteria and deadlines require manual verification.

title: Vice-Chancellor's International Excellence Scholarship
manual_review_requirements: Confirm the applicant is enrolling at the University of Waikato for the first time.
Confirm the applicant has applied for or received an offer for an eligible undergraduate or taught postgraduate programme.
Confirm the application is submitted before the deadline for the applicant's intended trimester.
Confirm the applicant demonstrates excellent academic achievement under the official selection process.
eligibility_text: The University of Waikato Vice-Chancellor's International Excellence Scholarship provides up to NZD 15,000 towards tuition fees for talented international students enrolling for the first time in an undergraduate or taught postgraduate programme. It is available across all study areas. Applications are reviewed monthly and use different deadlines for different trimesters, so no single deadline is recorded.

title: University of Manitoba Graduate Fellowship
manual_review_requirements: Confirm the applicant has achieved the official minimum admission GPA of 3.0 and any higher departmental threshold; the source page does not state the GPA scale.
Confirm the applicant is admitted to an eligible University of Manitoba master's or PhD programme and is not a pre-master's or occasional student.
Confirm the graduate programme is eligible for Tri-Agency funding.
Confirm the applicant's department or unit recommends the applicant for the fellowship.
Confirm the applicant will be registered full-time and progressing satisfactorily in the recommending unit.
Confirm the applicant satisfies the award-history, account-hold, grade, and concurrent-funding restrictions.
eligibility_text: The University of Manitoba Graduate Fellowship supports eligible master's and PhD students in graduate fields that qualify for Tri-Agency funding. Master's awards are valued at CAD 20,000 per year and doctoral awards at CAD 25,000 per year. All citizenships may apply. The page states a minimum admission GPA of 3.0 but does not identify its scale, so the numeric threshold remains a manual-review condition. Application timing and nomination are managed by departments or units.

title: Maastricht University NL-High Potential Scholarship
eligible_nationalities: Applicants from outside the European Union
manual_review_requirements: Confirm the applicant holds an eligible nationality outside the EU/EEA and satisfies the applicable Netherlands visa and residence-permit requirements.
Confirm the applicant applied to a participating full-time Maastricht University master's programme and meets its admission requirements.
Confirm the applicant has not participated in a degree-seeking higher-education programme in the Netherlands, except for permitted exchange study.
Confirm the applicant is not older than 35 years on 1 September 2026.
Review the official preference for applicants who have not already obtained a master's degree.
Confirm the applicant accepts the scholarship ambassador commitment.
Confirm all required documents and programme-admission steps were completed by their official deadlines.
eligibility_text: The Maastricht University NL-High Potential programme offers full scholarships including a tuition-fee waiver and monthly stipend for high-achieving applicants from outside the European Union who are admitted to a participating master's programme. Applicants must present a GPA of at least 7.5 on a 10.0 scale and must not be older than 35 on 1 September 2026. The scholarship deadline is 1 February 2026.
